title: Detecting Disconnected Components in a Graph

statement.markdown: Given an undirected graph represented as an adjacency list, your task is to detect and return the number of disconnected components in the graph. A component is a subgraph where there is a path between every pair of vertices.
statement.inputFormat: The graph will be represented as a dictionary where each key is a node and its corresponding value is a list of its neighboring nodes.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return the number of disconnected components in the graph.

editorial.title: Detecting Disconnected Components in a Graph Editorial
editorial.intuition: Perform DFS from an arbitrary node. Each time you visit a node that is not visited yet, mark its parent, increment the component count, and continue DFS.
editorial.betterSolution: Use DFS to find the first unvisited node and start exploring from there.
editorial.optimalSolution: DFS is the most suitable algorithm for this task. Perform DFS from an arbitrary node and count the number of connected components.
editorial.dryRun: Perform DFS from an arbitrary node and mark the visited nodes.
